title: Spoonfuls — Pudding #182: The Shop That Fixed My Son's Car
founder_voice_quotes: My son's car broke down. The shop sent a tow truck, bought the parts, put a tech on it, and we picked up the car the same day. No invoice. No deposit. No credit application.
The most durable economic relationships in your life don't require a credit score.

title: Spoonfuls — Pudding #183: The Triple Double and the Lottery Ticket Monkeys
founder_voice_quotes: The willingness to choose today to be motivated — when nothing around you is providing any — is the single rarest thing I've seen in adults.
$100 a day. Five days a week. Forty-eight weeks a year. That's $24,000. Then double it three times.

title: Skipping Stones — Pudding #182: The Shop That Fixed My Son's Car
founder_voice_quotes: I wired a shop's network for nine months. Never took a dollar. They keep my family's cars running.

title: Skipping Stones — Pudding #183: The Triple Double and the Lottery Ticket Monkeys
founder_voice_quotes: $100 a day. Five days a week. Forty-eight weeks. Then double it three times.
You have $2,000 saved. The stock market gives you $140 in a year. A business you run gives you a life you chose on purpose.

title: BST Episode — The Marks Loop That Closed Today
founder_voice_quotes: My son drove his car home today because I wired a network nine months ago. That's Marks. That's how it works. That's the whole thing.

title: BST Episode — The Triple Double and the Choice to Be Motivated
founder_voice_quotes: Who motivates the motivator? The motivator does.
You earn your way to the starting line. Then when you step onto it, you already know you can win the race.
